zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph Thompson於大約1560年出生在英國賀特福爾順特時，他的父親Maurice Thompson31歲，母親Katherine Harvey26歲。他於1596年2月2日與Elizabeth Harsnett在英國英格蘭肯特哈利善姆結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於從1634年到1648年在英國賀特福爾華頓 亞特 史東。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ralph Thompson于大约1560年出生在英国賀特福爾順特时，他的父亲Maurice Thompson31岁，母亲Katherine Harvey26岁。他于1596年2月2日与Elizabeth Harsnett在英国英格兰肯特哈利善姆结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他去世于从1634年到1648年在英国賀特福爾華頓 亞特 史東。

nameMeanings.name: Thompson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name T(h)om(me) (see Thom ) + -son ‘son of Tom’. Thomson is usually the Scottish form, that with the intrusive -p- being English. Both forms are common in Ireland. The surname Thompson is also very common among African Americans.
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German Thomsen and of its Swedish cognate Thomsson. Compare Thomson .

variants.name: Thomson
meanings: Scottish, English and northern Irish: variant of   Thompson  .
Scottish: sometimes an Anglicized form of Gaelic McThomas; see McTavish , McComish .
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German   Thomsen  . Compare   Thompson  .

variants.name: Thom
meanings: Scottish and English: from the Middle English personal name T(h)om(me), a pet form of Thomas . In Scotland, Thom is also an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thom, see McComb .
German: from the personal name Thom, a pet form of Thomas . This surname is also found in France.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 譚, see   Tan   1.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph Thompson was born about 1560, in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, England, his father, Maurice Thompson, was 31 and his mother, Katherine Harvey, was 26. He married Elizabeth Harsnett on 2 February 1596, in Harrietsham, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 8 daughters. He died from 1634 to 1648, in Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, England.

Il a épousé Elizabeth Harsnett le 2 février 1596, à Harrietsham, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé du 1634 au 1648, à Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph Thompson(이)가 대략 1560년 영국 허트퍼드실르 체슈운트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Maurice Thompson(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Harvey(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1596년 2월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 해리츠함에서 Elizabeth Harsnett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1634년 부터 1648년 까지 영국 허트퍼드실르 왓튼 앳 스톤에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph Thompson nasceu aproximadamente 1560, em Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Maurice Thompson, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Harvey, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Harsnett em 2 de fevereiro de 1596, em Harrietsham, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu de 1634 a 1648, em Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Sheston Chestnut, Watton-at-Stone, Hertf: ваттон ат стоун, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph Thompson родился около 1560 г. в местности чешант, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Maurice Thompson было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Katherine Harvey, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Harsnett 2 февраля 1596 г. в местности харриецхам, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Он умер с 1634 по 1648 в местности ваттон ат стоун, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ralph Thompson nació aproximadamente 1560, en Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Maurice Thompson, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Katherine Harvey, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Harsnett el 2 de febrero de 1596, en Harrietsham, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió desde 1634 hasta 1648, en Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
